title: Apple Raises Prices on Macs and iPads Amid the A.I. Boom
summary: The tech giant cited the soaring costs of memory and storage chips as it increased prices more than $200 on some devices.

title: IBM Unveils New Method to Make Smaller Computer Chip Parts
summary: Industry leaders had worried that innovations in chip miniaturization were no longer possible.

title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil Custom A.I. Chip Design
summary: The maker of ChatGPT plans to use enough chips to consume 10 gigawatts of electricity, an amount that could power millions of households.

title: Meta Has Created a Prediction Markets App
summary: The experimental app, internally called “Arena,” would be independent of Facebook and Instagram. It could compete for attention with Polymarket and Kalshi, the biggest prediction markets.

title: The New York Times Amends Lawsuit Against OpenAI and Microsoft
summary: In a new court filing, The Times accused Microsoft of encouraging OpenAI to train its A.I. systems using copyrighted articles.

title: OpenAI Leans Toward Holding Up I.P.O. Until Next Year
summary: The A.I. company’s advisers are pushing its chief executive, Sam Altman, to move slowly after SpaceX’s stock has been volatile and as the start-up grapples with financial challenges.

title: Chinese A.I. Models Gain Ground on Anthropic and OpenAI
summary: Silicon Valley engineers recently flocked to new technology from a Chinese company, Z.ai, that is almost as good as its American competitors but much cheaper.

title: Larry Sanger, a Wikipedia Founder, Is Barred From Editing on the Site
summary: A vote to bar Larry Sanger indefinitely from the volunteer community of Wikipedia came days after he submitted a proposal on “intellectual diversity.”

title: Your Home Could Help Solve AI’s Growing Power Demand
summary: Tesla, Sunrun and Renew Home plan to tap solar panels, batteries, thermostats and other devices installed in millions of homes to meet the energy demands of artificial intelligence.

title: N.S.A. Lost Access to Powerful A.I. Model Amid Anthropic Dispute
summary: A recent episode underscored the Trump administration’s increasing reliance on advanced A.I. systems for cybersecurity even as it battles a leading U.S. developer.

title: China Takes Supercomputer Crown From U.S. For First Time Since 2017
summary: A supercomputer in Shenzhen was declared the world’s fastest. It uses only standard microprocessors and not the special-purpose chips called graphics processing units.

title: Tesla Crash That Killed a Texas Woman Will be Investigated by Federal Regulators
summary: The car’s driver-assistance system was in use when the crash killed a woman on Friday, the police said.

title: Tesla Driver Using Autopilot Crashes Into Home in Texas and Kills a Woman, Officials Say
summary: The driver told investigators he was using the vehicle’s automated-driver system when the vehicle left the roadway and crashed into a house in Harris County, Texas.

title: A Humble 3-Wheel Electric Vehicle Lands Toyota in Federal Court
summary: A lawsuit filed in California claims the automaker’s philanthropic arm stole technology intended to help poor farmers, but it is not clear to what end.

title: ‘Hard Fork’ Live, Part 3: Differing Visions of an A.I. Future
summary: One final episode from our live event, featuring a debate, questions from listeners and the dramatic and unplanned collapse of a dancing robot.

title: The Ageless and Undefeated Champs of Tulsa’s Wii Bowling League
summary: The U.V. Okies have dominated a competition with other senior living facilities for six seasons, but they’re not just there to win.

title: Have You Bought a Tesla Recently? We Want to Hear From You.
summary: Tesla sales are rebounding in Europe even though many people have negative feelings about Elon Musk, the company’s chief executive. We want to know why.

title: Amazon Retaliated Against Workers Who Supported Regulating Data Centers, Complaint Says
summary: The employees encouraged limits on the complexes in a series of hearings in the tech giant’s hometown, Seattle.

title: Trump Says Apple to Buy Computer Chips from Intel
summary: While neither company has publicly discussed the deal, it would be a big break for Intel, which sold a 10 percent stake to the U.S. government last year.

title: Tech Workers Maxed Out Their A.I. Use. Now They’re Trying to Minimize It.
summary: Artificial intelligence is expensive to use, many companies discovered. That has led to a new era of saving costs.
